results.description: DESCRIPTION:THE PURPOSE OF THIS PROJECT IS TO DEVELOP AN ENERGY-JUSTICE MODEL FOR FREIGHT VEHICLE ELECTRIFICATION IN AN URBAN ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE COMMUNITY THAT IS RESPONSIVE TO COMMUNITY NEEDS WHILE ADVANCING SCIENCE IN AIR QUALITY, PUBLIC HEALTH, SOCIOECONOMIC, ORGANIZATIONAL, AND INSTITUTIONAL FACTORS THAT PLAY A ROLE IN ASSESSING BENEFITS AND COSTS OF TRANSITIONING INTO FREIGHT VEHICLE ELECTRIFICATION.ACTIVITIES:THE SPECIFIC OBJECTIVES ARE: 1) ANALYZE THE SOCIOTECHNICAL REGIME IN WHICH A TRANSITION TO ELECTRIFIED FREIGHT VEHICLES WILL OCCUR; 2) EVALUATE POTENTIAL SCENARIOS FOR SUCH A TRANSITION IN TERMS OF THEIR REDUCTION IN AIR EMISSIONS; AND 3) DETERMINE THE HEALTH BENEFITS OF SUCH A TRANSITION FOR A COMMUNITY THAT IS HEAVILY IMPACTED BY FREIGHT VEHICLE TRAFFIC. SUBRECIPIENT:SUBAWARDEE UNIVERSITY OF ILLINOIS CHICAGO WILL LEAD THE WORK ON BASELINE EXPOSURE ASSESSMENT, ELECTRIFICATION SCENARIO ANALYSIS OF EMISSION MODELING, AIR DISPERSION MODELING, AND EXPOSURE AND HEALTH IMPACTS ASSESSMENT. UNIVERSITY OF ILLINOIS CHICAGO WILL ALSO BE INVOLVED IN RECRUITING COMMUNITY PARTICIPANTS, MOBILE SENSOR TRAINING, AND BASELINE AIR MONITORING DATA COLLECTION.OUTCOMES:DELIVERABLES INCLUDE ANNUAL AND FINAL RESEARCH REPORTS. OTHER EXPECTED DELIVERABLES INCLUDE A COMMUNITY AIR MONITORING AND EXPOSURE ASSESSMENT PLAN (AMEAP); COMMUNITY AIR MONITORING GUIDANCE MATERIALS FOR LOW-COST SENSORS; A DATA REPOSITORY THAT CONTAINS A VARIETY OF ENVIRONMENTAL AND TRAFFIC DATA COLLECTED BY THE PROJECT; A HOLISTIC ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT ANALYSIS TOOL KIT; AND AN ONLINE MAPPING TOOL FOR COMMUNITY STAKEHOLDERS. EXPECTED OUTCOMES ARE IMPROVED ABILITY TO PREDICT THE POTENTIAL PUBLIC HEALTH AND AIR QUALITY RISK TRADEOFFS IN UNDERSERVED COMMUNITIES DUE TO THE FREIGHT ELECTRIFICATION-RELATED ENERGY TRANSITIONS; AND ENHANCED SOCIOECONOMIC, CULTURAL, AND BEHAVIORAL INSIGHTS THAT CAN INFORM FUTURE ELECTRIFICATION SCENARIOS. DIRECT BENEFICIARIES OF THIS PROJECT INCLUDE THE RESIDENTS OF THE LITTLE VILLAGE NEIGHBORHOOD IN SOUTHWEST CHICAGO AND SIMILAR COMMUNITIES SITUATED NEAR FREIGHT RAILS AS WELL AS RESEARCHERS WORKING WITH SIMILAR COMMUNITIES TO ADDRESS ENVIRONMENTAL CONCERNS ASSOCIATED WITH LARGE-SCALE CHANGES IN ENERGY AND TRANSPORTATION SYSTEMS.
